english: dpáṅs spyód-pa (dpangs spyod pa) Mil., to cross the height of the heavens, to fly across the sky. — nam-gru (nam gru) v. rgyu-skar (rgyu skar).

pronounced *nám-da (nam da), and nám-la (nam la)*, Mil., Pth., col., season, nám-zla dus bži (nam zla dus bzhi) the four seasons; da nam-da ston šar (da nam da ston shar) now autumn has set in; *da nam-da ḍaṅ-mo soṅ (da nam da ḍang mo song)*; fig. nám-da _das (nam da _das) the (favourable) season has passed Mil.
v. na (na) I. and II., 2; also ná-k'a (na kha).

adj., and nár-mar (nar mar) adv., continuous, without interruption Sch.; *či-ma nár-te ṭon or šor* (*ci ma nar te ṭon or shor*) C. torrents of tears gushed from his eyes, cf. k'rul (khrul); nár-re (nar re) Mil., more vulg. *nár-ra-ra* (*nar ra ra*) in a long row or file, _grúl-ba (_grul ba) to walk.
oblong Mil., Med.; k'a-nar-čan (kha nar can) having the shape of a rectangle; gru-nar-čan (gru nar can) rhombic, lozenge-shaped. Cf. (b)snár-ba ((b)snar ba).
n. of a precious stone Sch.
Cs. incest, fornication; nal-grib (nal grib) pollution by it. nal-p'rúg (nal phrug) frq., *nal-lě* (*nal le*) Ts., bastard-child; nál-bu (nal bu) Sch. a libidinous woman (??).

I. sbst. 1. barley, in three varieties: mgyógs-nas (mgyogs nas) (Ld. yáṅ-ma (yang ma), or drug-ču-nas (drug cu nas), Wdṅ. k'rá-ma (khra ma)) early barley, ripening in about 60 days; sér-mo (ser mo) late barley, the best sort; č'e-nas (che nas) a middling sort. — 2. barley-corn, nas-tsam (nas tsam) as much as a barley-corn Glr. — nás-č'aṅ (nas chang) beer brewed of barley. nas-rjén (nas rjen) v. rjén-pa (rjen pa). — nas-p'yé (nas phye) barley-flour. — *nas-zír* (*nas zir*) (spelling not certain) aim or sight on a gun W. II. postp., sign of the ablative case (almost like las (las)) 1. added to sbst.: from, byáṅ-p'yogs-nas (byang phyogs nas) from the north, often joined with bzuṅ-ste (bzung ste) (Ld. *táṅs-te* (*tangs te*)), commencing from, extending from, with a following to, as far as; till, until, with respect so space and time; by, lág-pa-nas _dzín-pa (lag pa nas _dzin pa) or _jú-ba (_ju ba) to take a person by the hand, miṅ-nas rjód-pa (ming nas rjod pa), smó-ba (smo ba) to call by name, tigs-nas pa re-ré-nas (tigs nas pa re re nas) (to count) by single drops, so-só-nas (so so nas) one by one, each by himself; through, duṅ-nas bšád-pas (dung nas bshad pas) speaking through a trumpet Glr., sgo-sáṅ-nas ltá-ba (sgo sang nas lta ba) looking through the chink of a door Tar.; sgó-nas gtóṅ-ba (sgo nas gtong ba) to admit through the door Dzl.; *bi-yaṅ-ne p'aṅ* (*bi yang ne phang*) W. he flung it through the hole (cf. also rgyúd-pa (rgyud pa) I., 2); made, manufactured, built etc. of, pá-gu-nas (pa gu nas) of bricks; (made, worked, struck etc.) with, *lág-pa-ne duṅ* (*lag pa ne dung*) W. struck with the hand; denoting distance: rgyaṅ-grágs gčig-nas pó-ta-la yod (rgyang grags gcig nas po ta la yod) C., Potala lies within reach of the ear; _dí-nas gáṅs-ri-la (_di nas gangs ri la) far from here on the snowy mountain Glr.; with respect to time: after, žag bdún-nas (zhag bdun nas) after seven days: dé-nas (de nas) after that, afterwards, then. — 2. added to verbs, as gerundial particle, rarely to the inf., gen. (col. always) to the verbal root, prop. after, since; also equivalent to te (te), when added to a pres. or pf. root (instances of which are to be met with almost on every page of Tibetan books); together with _dug (_dug) or yod (yod) added to a pres. or pf. tense, col. frq., in B. rarely: ṅa lčeb dgos snyám-nas yod (nga lceb dgos snyam nas yod) I think I must seek death Pth.; tsós-nas yod (tsos nas yod) it is boiled Pth.; só-nam-gyi byá-ba-la žúgs-nas yód-pa-la (so nam gyi bya ba la zhugs nas yod pa la) as they began to till the ground Glr. — Col. also for na (na).

I. 1. particle, col. also *niṅ* (*ning*); Cs. justly remarks: 'an emphatical particle', serving to give force to that word or part of a sentence, which rhetorically is most important, esp. also (though not exclusively, Sch.) to separate the subject of a sentence from its predicate, thus adding to perspicuity: kyod _dir _óṅs-pa ni ṅai mťus _óṅs-so (kyod _dir _ongs pa ni ngai mthus _ongs so) thy coming hither has been effected by my (magic) power Dzl.; bdag ni brám-ze yin (bdag ni bram ze yin) myself am a Brahmin Dzl.; de ni ṅa yin (de ni nga yin) that one am I; _dí ni mi pód-do (_di ni mi pod do) this I am not able to do Dzl.; ťá-mál-pa ni ma yin (tha mal pa ni ma yin) a vulgar person she is not Dzl.; des ni (des ni) it is by this (that...); stobs ni (stobs ni) as to strength (I...); gál-te nús-na ni (gal te nus na ni) if he
